name: Ranged Attack
description: An attack that applies damage or effects from a distance through projectiles, traces, beams, or targeting rules.
design_notes: Ranged attacks need rules for aiming, ammunition, projectile behavior, line of sight, cover, falloff, and hit confirmation. Decide whether the attack is hitscan, simulated projectile, lock-on, arced, or area-targeted; each choice changes readability and counterplay.
design_purpose: Let players project threat beyond melee reach and interact with distant targets.
Create spatial decisions around cover, reloads, aim commitment, and projectile travel.
Support weapon identity through spread, recoil, charge, ammo, and impact effects.
player_fantasy: The player feels capable of controlling space and landing precise attacks at range.

parameters.name: projectile_spawn_offset
parameters.description: Spawn position relative to the firing actor.

parameters.name: aim_spread_degrees
parameters.description: Angular dispersion applied to each shot.

parameters.name: damage_falloff_start_distance
parameters.description: Distance where damage reduction begins if falloff is used.

parameters.name: cover_trace_radius
parameters.description: Collision radius used by projectile or hitscan cover checks.

edge_cases: Firing near cover should not spawn projectiles behind walls or inside the shooter's collision.
Very fast projectiles need continuous collision or sub-stepping to avoid tunneling.
Projectile ownership must handle reflection, friendly fire, despawn, and save/load boundaries.
Aim assist, lock-on, recoil, and manual aim should not fight over the final shot direction.
common_bugs: Projectile visuals and damage traces use different origins, causing visible misses to hit.
Ammo is consumed before server rejection and not refunded after prediction correction.
Spread uses frame-random state and breaks deterministic replays or seeded runs.
Projectiles continue after scene unload because lifetime cleanup is tied to renderer visibility.
balancing_notes: Long reach needs counter-pressure such as reload, ammo, spread, travel, cover, recoil, or enemy mobility.
Projectile travel gives defenders readable counterplay; hitscan needs stronger aim or exposure constraints.
accessibility_notes: Offer reticle scaling, color options, aim assist settings, and clear impact feedback where precision aiming is central.
Use hit markers or impact effects so players can distinguish misses, armor hits, blocked shots, and immune targets.

implementation_notes.unity: Validate ammo on the weapon state before firing, spawn from the muzzle transform or camera-corrected origin, and assign projectile_id, owner_id, and damage metadata from a pooled projectile.
implementation_notes.godot: Use weapon state for ammo authority, then either raycast hitscan from the muzzle/camera rule or instance a projectile scene with owner_id, projectile_id, and lifetime metadata.
implementation_notes.unreal: Consume ammo on the server or predicted ability path, fire hitscan traces or replicated projectile actors from a muzzle socket, and keep projectile ownership for reflection and kill credit.
implementation_notes.web: Commit a fire transaction that checks ammo, computes final spread seed, and creates hitscan results or projectile entities with projectile_id, owner_id, spawn transform, and collision radius.

relationships.reason: Ranged impacts benefit from hit confirmation when targets are distant, occluded, or partly outside the camera focus.
Reflected projectiles need ownership and damage attribution rules so ranged attacks cannot double-hit incorrectly.

example_games.title: Doom
example_games.note: Uses varied ranged weapons with distinct projectile and hitscan behavior.

example_games.title: Gears of War
example_games.note: Uses ranged weapon fire as the main combat interaction in cover-based encounters.

example_games.title: Grand Theft Auto V
example_games.note: Uses firearms and other ranged weapons as common combat interactions.

example_games.title: Counter-Strike 2
example_games.note: Uses precise ranged attacks as the core of round-based firefights.

example_games.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
example_games.note: Uses crossbow shots as a visible ranged combat option.

example_games.title: Overwatch
example_games.note: Uses many ranged attack models including projectiles, beams, and scoped shots.

example_games.title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
example_games.note: Uses bows, spells, and projectiles as visible ranged combat options.

example_games.title: Resident Evil 4
example_games.note: Uses firearms as the primary ranged combat interaction.

example_games.title: Fortnite
example_games.note: Uses firearms and projectile weapons as core combat interactions.

example_games.title: Apex Legends
example_games.note: Uses firearms and abilities as core ranged combat interactions.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Uses ranged weapons and cast-like attacks as visible combat options.

example_games.title: Left 4 Dead
example_games.note: Uses firearms as the primary ranged combat interaction against infected hordes.

example_games.title: Diablo III
example_games.note: Uses ranged class skills and projectiles as visible combat options.

example_games.title: Half-Life 2
example_games.note: Uses firearms and projectile weapons as visible first-person combat options.

example_games.title: World of Warcraft
example_games.note: Uses ranged spells, weapons, and abilities across combat roles.

example_games.title: Spelunky
example_games.note: Uses thrown objects and ranged weapons as visible combat options.

example_games.title: Dead Cells
example_games.note: Uses bows, traps, and ranged weapons as visible combat options.

example_games.title: Super Metroid
example_games.note: Uses beam and missile attacks as visible ranged combat tools.

example_games.title: Terraria
example_games.note: Uses bows, guns, magic, and thrown weapons as visible ranged combat options.

example_games.title: Dishonored
example_games.note: Uses crossbow and pistol tools as visible ranged combat options.
